sutta: once when the exalted 1 dwelt near champa on the banks of lake gagara a company of lay disciples from champa visited the venerable sarikuta saluted him and sat down at 1 side and seated thus they said to him it's been long reverend sir since we heard the dhamma talk from the exalted 1's mouth how good it would be to do so and verbal sarikuta then come next upusata day friends and maybe you will hear 1 yes reverend sir they rejoined and getting up the lady cycles from champa saluted the variable sariputta and departed keeping him on their right
commentary: i just stopped here to commend them the uposata are the days of the lunar calendar the eighth day the 15th day the 23rd and the last day in the 30th or if there's no third year then the 29th day of the lunar calendar these are the days when the lay people would keep the 8 precepts and uh monks would also give dharma talks on those days and the you notice when the lay people departed now they kept the right shoulder to the verbal sarikuta this is an indian tradition and you show respect you you your right shoulder faces the monk and that is why also our mounts we sit now with the senior monk on our right and also we bear our right shoulder this is all indian custom and the suta continues now when the day was come the approach the vulnerable 1 saluted and stood at 1 side and with them the verbal sariputta visited the exalted 1 saluted and sat down at 1 side so seated he said to the exalted 1 lord may a man's gift be given here in such a way as to become not great in fruit grey in profit and may the gift be given in such a way as to become grey in fruit great in profit it may be sariputa and what law is the reason the cause consider sarikuta a self-seeking man wrapped up in the result seeking reward who gives a gift of thinking i'll enjoy this hereafter and to recluses and brahmins gives this gift food drink clothing vehicle garlands perfumes ointments bed dwelling lighting but thank you sariputta might a man give here such a gift yes lord now he who gives thus wise hariputta thereafter on the breaking up of the body after death arises in the company of the 4 royal devas and when he has exhausted the power glory dominion he becomes a returner a comer to this state here then consider 1 who gives a gift but is no self-seeker not wrapped up in the result seeking no reward nor things to enjoy the fruit hereafter yet he gives thinking it's good to give or 1 who thinks not so as he gives but thinks this was given in the past done in the past by my father and my father's father i ought not to allow this ancient family to accustomed to labs or he thinks not so but he thinks as those sages of or had those great offerings so will i make this arms distribution or he thinks not so but thinks this gift of mine calms the mind and joy and gladness arise or he thinks not so but he gives a gift to improve the mind to equip the mind and to recluses and brahmanas he gives this gift food and so forth what thank you sarikuta might a man give here such a gift yes lord now he who gives thus wise thereafter on the breaking up of the body after death arises in the company of the devas of brahma's host and when he has exhausted that deep power glory dominion he becomes a non-returner a common not to this state here verily this is the reason the cause why a man's gift given in 1 way may become not great in fruit great in profit and this is the reason the cause why given in such a way the gift may become grey in fruit great in profit and that's the end of the sultan so here this is saying when we give a gift we should not think of the result we should not think of enjoying the result of that gift we should gift without a selfish partner just because it is good to give and because if a person acts with a selfish daughter then he becomes a returner he comes back to this world again but if he behaves without himself even when he gives he does nothing of any reward for himself because he acts like an arya then eventually he becomes an arya we know in these some other sutas the buddha said always to see the body and the mind is not i not mind not myself and we have to learn to act like an arya and slowly will become an arya the next suta is
